context: The final 1.x release before v2.0. Bridges the gap between local environment management and production DevOps workflows. Adds container integration and a template system so teams can standardize env setups across projects.

tasks.title: Docker Compose Integration
tasks.description: Auto-sync changes made to .env into the environment section of docker-compose.yml. When the user switches environments or edits .env, detect which services in docker-compose.yml reference that .env file and update them. Use a file watcher to detect docker-compose.yml changes and warn the user of drift.
features: Parse docker-compose.yml to find env_file references
Detect which services use each .env file
Warn when .env and docker-compose are out of sync
Optional: auto-update docker-compose environment section on switch
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Changes to .env are reflected in docker-compose.yml within 1 second. Sync warning shows correctly.

tasks.title: Container Environment Export
tasks.description: Add export commands to convert the current .env file into container-ready formats. Support three output formats: Docker run -e flags (for manual docker run commands), env_file format (already standard .env), and a shell export script (export VAR=value for each line).
tasks.acceptance_criteria: All three export formats generate valid output. docker run command runs successfully with exported flags.

tasks.title: Kubernetes ConfigMap and Secret Generation
tasks.description: Generate Kubernetes resource files from the current .env. Non-sensitive variables go into a ConfigMap. Variables flagged as secrets (detected by the ML model or named with SECRET/KEY/TOKEN/PASSWORD) go into a Kubernetes Secret with proper base64 encoding. Output as valid YAML files.
outputs: configmap.yaml — non-sensitive variables as string data
secret.yaml — sensitive variables as base64-encoded data
tasks.classification_logic: Use ML model confidence + variable name patterns to split sensitive vs non-sensitive
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Generated YAML applies cleanly with kubectl apply. Base64 values decode correctly.

tasks.title: Template Creation Command
tasks.description: Add a 'Create Template from Current .env' command. The user is prompted to: give the template a name, mark each variable as required or optional, add a human-readable description per variable, and choose whether to include the current value or replace with a placeholder. Output saved as .env.template in workspace root.

template_format.example:
# Required: Stripe secret key for payment processing
# Type: string
# Example: sk_live_...
STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=

# Optional: Enable debug logging
# Type: boolean
# Default: false
DEBUG=false

tasks.acceptance_criteria: Template file created in under 1 second. Required/optional markers and descriptions preserved.

tasks.title: Built-in Template Library
tasks.description: Ship a curated library of environment templates for 20+ popular technology stacks. Each template includes all commonly needed variables with descriptions and example values. Accessible via a 'New from Template' command in the extension.
tasks.acceptance_criteria: All 20+ templates load correctly. Variables have accurate descriptions and example values.

tasks.title: Template Import and Export
tasks.description: Import templates from: local .env.template files, GitHub Gist URL (public), or a dotenvy.json manifest. Export current .env as a shareable template to: local file, clipboard (for pasting into GitHub Gist), or a team-shared location via Doppler.
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Import/export round-trip preserves all variable names, descriptions, and required/optional markers. GitHub Gist import works with public URLs.

tasks.title: Template Validation
tasks.description: Add a 'Validate Against Template' command. Given a .env.template file, check the current .env for: missing required variables (error), missing optional variables (warning), extra variables not in the template (info), and type mismatches (e.g. PORT should be a number). Show results in a dedicated panel or Problems tab.

validation_levels.warning: Missing optional variable or wrong type
validation_levels.info: Extra variable not defined in template

tasks.acceptance_criteria: Validation catches all missing required variables. Results appear in VS Code Problems panel.

tasks.title: Team Template Sync via Doppler
tasks.description: Extend the existing Doppler cloud sync to support template sharing. When a team member runs pull, they also receive the latest .env.template for the project. On push, the template file is included. This ensures every team member works from the same environment specification.
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Template file included in push/pull operations. New team member receives correct template on first pull.

tasks.title: Container Environment Detection
tasks.description: Detect when VS Code is running inside a Dev Container or Docker container. When detected: adjust file path resolution for .env files, warn the user that direct file writes may not persist, suggest using volume mounts or environment injection instead.
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Container environment detected correctly. User sees appropriate warning and guidance.

priority_guide.P0: Must have — core value of this release
priority_guide.P2: Nice to have — include if time permits

success_criteria: Docker export validated successfully with docker-compose up
Kubernetes YAML applies cleanly with kubectl apply
Template library covers 20+ stacks with accurate variable definitions
Template import/export round-trip preserves all metadata
Validation catches all missing required variables
